# CC Director / DevThrottle Tool Review - Shared Brief

This brief drives a double review of every command-line tool in this repository.
Two independent reviewers - one Claude Code session and one Codex session - each
work through this same brief and write their own findings. We then compare the
two, region by region, and decide what to keep, expand, document, change ship
status on, merge, or retire.

Both reviewers MUST use the rubric and output format below so the two result
sets line up tool for tool.

## Hard rules

- ASCII characters only. No Unicode, no emojis, no smart quotes, no box-drawing.
  This is a strict project rule for all output, including these review files.
- Plain English. Spell out words; avoid abbreviations and jargon.
- Do not change any tool, registry entry, or shipped behavior during the review.
  This pass is assessment only. Findings can recommend changes; they do not make
  them.

## Source of truth

- The list of tools and their ship status lives in `tools/registry.json`. A tool
  ships in the installed product only when its entry has `"ship": true`.
- The build pipeline `scripts/build-python-bundle.ps1` reads that file and
  bundles only the shipped tools.
- Each tool's source lives under `tools/<name>/`. Some have a `README.md`, many
  do not.
- Existing combined docs: `docs/CC_TOOLS.md` (older, mixes shipped and
  non-shipped, partly stale), `docs/cli-reference.md` (auto-generated from
  --help), `docs/public/tools/01-overview.md` (thin public overview), and the new
  per-tool pages under `docs/tools/` (shipped tools only).
- On an installed machine the shipped tools appear as shim files under
  `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\bin\` and on the PATH.

## Inventory - 42 tools in 7 regions

`*` marks a tool that ships by default today (`"ship": true`).

### Region 1 - Documents (5)
cc-pdf*, cc-html*, cc-word*, cc-excel, cc-powerpoint

### Region 2 - Email (2)
cc-gmail*, cc-outlook*

### Region 3 - Fleet messaging (6)
cc-sessions*, cc-whoami*, cc-send*, cc-ask*, cc-spawn*, cc-fleet-selftest*

### Region 4 - Data and utilities (9)
cc-vault*, cc-comm-queue*, cc-settings*, cc-cron*, cc-docgen, cc-hardware,
cc-personresearch, cc-posthog, cc-director-setup

### Region 5 - Web and social (10)
cc-playwright*, cc-browser, cc-crawl4ai, cc-websiteaudit,
cc-brandingrecommendations, cc-reddit, cc-spotify, cc-twitter, cc-facebook,
cc-youtube

### Region 6 - Media (7)
cc-image, cc-photos, cc-video, cc-voice, cc-whisper, cc-transcribe,
cc-youtube-info

### Region 7 - Desktop automation (3)
cc-click, cc-computer, cc-trisight

## Per-tool rubric

For every tool, answer all of the following:

1. Purpose and benefit - what the tool is for and the value it delivers.
2. Primary consumer - who calls it: the user directly, an agent or session, a
   specific skill, or an internal service. Name the consumer if you can find it.
3. Works now - is it installed and does it run. Check that the source under
   `tools/<name>/` is present, that an entry point exists, and where possible run
   `cc-<name> --help` (shipped tools have a shim at
   `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\bin\cc-<name>.cmd`; for non-shipped tools inspect
   the source entry point instead). State a confidence level: confirmed working,
   probably working, unknown, or broken.
4. In use - search the repository for references: under `.claude/skills/`,
   `CLAUDE.md`, C# source in `src/`, and `scripts/`. Is it alive (something calls
   it) or dead (no references)? List the strongest reference you find.
5. Overlap - does it duplicate or substantially overlap another tool. Name the
   other tool and say which should win.
6. Ship status - current value in the registry, and what it should be.
7. Documentation - none, partial, or good. Point at the doc if one exists.
8. Verdict - exactly one primary verdict from this set, plus a one-line
   rationale and a concrete suggested action:
   - KEEP - needed, works, ship status correct; little or no change.
   - EXPAND - valuable but underbuilt; invest more (features and depth).
   - DOCUMENT - kept as is, only needs documentation.
   - PROMOTE - should start shipping (set ship to true).
   - DEMOTE - should stop shipping but stay in the repo (set ship to false).
   - MERGE - fold into another named tool.
   - RETIRE - no longer needed; turn off and plan removal.

## Output format

Write one findings file. Group by the 7 regions in order. Under each region,
one block per tool in this exact shape:

```
### cc-name
- Purpose: ...
- Consumer: ...
- Works: <confirmed|probable|unknown|broken> - ...
- In use: <alive|dead|unclear> - strongest reference: ...
- Overlap: <none|tool name + who wins>
- Ship status: current=<true|false>, recommended=<true|false>
- Documentation: <none|partial|good> - ...
- Verdict: <KEEP|EXPAND|DOCUMENT|PROMOTE|DEMOTE|MERGE|RETIRE> - rationale
- Action: one concrete next step
```

End the file with a "Region summaries" section: for each region, a one-paragraph
take and a count of verdicts (how many KEEP, EXPAND, RETIRE, and so on).

## Known anomalies to investigate and report

- `cc-disk` is installed in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\cc-director\bin\` on at least one
  machine but does not appear in `tools/registry.json` at all. Find out what it
  is and whether it should be in the registry.
- `src/CcDirector.Core/Tools/tools-manifest.json` (the runtime health-check
  catalog) is missing `cc-cron`, even though `cc-cron` ships. The guard test
  `FleetToolsShipGuardTests` is supposed to keep the registry and the manifest in
  step.
- `docs/CC_TOOLS.md` documents cc-pdf, cc-html, and cc-word under a single
  fictional name "cc-markdown" that does not match the real commands.

## Output locations

- Claude Code session writes to: `docs/plans/tool-review/findings-claude.md`
- Codex session writes to: `docs/plans/tool-review/findings-codex.md`

After both files exist, the two sessions' owners compare them region by region.
